questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Registers in 8085:
options: A (accumulator), B, C, D, E, H, L (general purpose), SP, PC, flags
questions.solution: 8085 registers: 8-bit general (B,C,D,E,H,L) + 16-bit (SP, PC). Flags: S, Z, AC, P, CY.

questions.subject: Electronics and Telecommunication Engineering
questions.stem: Bidirectional DC-DC converter operates in:
questions.solution: Bidirectional: energy flows both ways. Buck mode: source to load. Boost mode: load to source. Regenerative braking.

questions.subject: Electronics and Telecommunication Engineering
questions.stem: LMS algorithm: convergence speed depends on:
options: Step size and input signal eigenvalues
questions.solution: Convergence: μ controls speed/stability tradeoff. Eigenvalue spread of input autocorrelation affects convergence rate.

questions.subject: Electronics and Telecommunication Engineering
questions.stem: Lossless transmission line: voltage standing wave ratio VSWR =
questions.solution: VSWR = (1+|Γ|)/(1-|Γ|). Γ = reflection coefficient. VSWR=1: matched. VSWR=∞: open or short.

questions.subject: Electronics and Telecommunication Engineering
questions.stem: DMA transfer: CPU is:
questions.solution: DMA: direct memory access without CPU intervention. CPU can execute other code while DMA transfers data.

questions.subject: Electronics and Telecommunication Engineering
questions.stem: Clamp-on ammeter: measures current by:
questions.solution: Clamp meter: transformer principle. Core clamps around conductor. Current induces secondary, measured by meter.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: BJT bias: Vcc=15V, Rb=100kΩ, Rc=4.7kΩ, β=300. Base current Ib:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Pulse modulation — PWM:
options: Pulse width varies with amplitude. Used in power control, DC motors
questions.solution: PWM: duty cycle = signal amplitude. Switching amplifiers. Class D audio.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A 10-bit ADC has a full-scale range of 5 V. Its resolution (LSB step) is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Second-order system: ω_n=5 rad/s, ζ=0.707. Percentage overshoot ≈

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: 8086 minimum vs maximum mode:
options: Minimum: small system (single processor). Maximum: large system (coprocessor, bus arbiter)
questions.solution: Minimum: IO/M, RD, WR pins. Maximum: S₀S₁S₂ status, uses 8288 bus controller.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Routh-Hurwitz: s⁴+1s³+5s²+6s+2=0. System is:
questions.solution: All coefficients positive and Routh array checks needed.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: 8086 segmented memory: segment registers CS, DS, SS, ES:
options: CS: code, DS: data, SS: stack, ES: extra data
questions.solution: Segment registers: CS (IP), DS (data), SS (SP/BP), ES (string operations, extra data).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Root locus starts at open-loop poles and ends at:
questions.solution: Root locus: branches start at OL poles, end at OL zeros or infinity.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: DMA (Direct Memory Access):
options: Peripheral ↔ memory without CPU. DMA controller. Cycle stealing or burst mode
questions.solution: DMA: 1+ channel. Transfer types: single, block, demand. Scatter-gather for physical memory.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Second-order system: ω_n=10 rad/s, ζ=0.3. Percentage overshoot ≈

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: 8086 segment:offset addressing: physical address =

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Poynting vector S = E×H represents:
questions.solution: S = E×H. Units: W/m². Time-average: S_avg = ½Re(E×H*).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A 12-bit ADC has a full-scale range of 5 V. Its resolution (LSB step) is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The program counter (PC) in a microprocessor holds the:
questions.solution: The PC points to the memory address of the next instruction to fetch.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Antenna radiation pattern:
options: Graphical representation of radiation intensity as function of direction
questions.solution: Pattern: isotropic (sphere), directional (beam), omnidirectional (donut). Main lobe, sidelobes, backlobe.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Synthesis in digital design:
options: HDL → netlist (gates + flip-flops). Constraints: timing, area, power. Technology mapping
questions.solution: Logic synthesis: RTL → gates. Optimization. Design compiler (Synopsys), Vivado, Quartus.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: 8086 instruction prefetch: instructions are fetched ahead of execution into:
options: Prefetch queue (6 bytes in 8086, 4 bytes in 8088)
questions.solution: 8086: 6-byte prefetch queue. 8088: 4-byte. Bus idle cycles used for prefetch.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Reflection coefficient Γ:
questions.solution: Γ = (Z_L-Z₀)/(Z_L+Z₀). |Γ| ≤ 1 for passive loads. Return loss = -20log|Γ|.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: K-map for 4-variable: f=Σm(0,1,4,5,8,9,12,13). Minimized:
questions.solution: All minterms have B=0. K-map shows B̅ covers all.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: CPU — RISC vs CISC:
options: RISC: simple instructions, pipelined, load-store. CISC: complex, variable length, memory ops
questions.solution: RISC: ARM, MIPS, RISC-V. CISC: x86. RISC simpler to pipeline. CISC denser code.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Magnetic vector potential A satisfies: ∇²A + k²A = -μJ (Helmholtz). Boundary conditions at conductor surface:
options: Tangential E and normal B are continuous
questions.solution: Boundary: E_tan1 = E_tan2, B_norm1 = B_norm2. For perfect conductor: E_tan=0, B_norm=0.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A varactor diode is used as:
questions.solution: Varactor: junction capacitance varies with reverse voltage. Used in VCO, tuners, phase shifters.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An AM signal carries a message of highest frequency 4 kHz. Its transmission bandwidth is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A signal has a maximum frequency of 8 kHz. Its Nyquist sampling rate is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The bandwidth of an AM signal modulated by a message of highest frequency fm is:
questions.solution: AM produces two sidebands, giving a transmission bandwidth of 2fm.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Laplace Transform of e^{-at}u(t):
questions.solution: Standard: L{e^{-at}u(t)} = 1/(s+a). Region of convergence: Re{s}>-a.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Routh-Hurwitz: s⁴+1s³+2s²+3s+6=0. System is:
questions.solution: All coefficients positive and Routh array checks needed.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Routh-Hurwitz: s⁴+1s³+4s²+5s+2=0. System is:
questions.solution: All coefficients positive and Routh array checks needed.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Root locus — breakaway points:
options: Where roots leave real axis. dK/ds = 0. Multiple roots
questions.solution: Breakaway: between two poles. Break-in: between two zeros. d(system eq)/ds = 0.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A 5-bit binary counter counts from:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: 16-QAM constellation:
options: 16 points in a 4×4 grid (4 bits per symbol)
questions.solution: QAM: combined amplitude and phase modulation. 16-QAM: 16 states, 4 bits/symbol.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Frequency-division multiplexing (FDM) shares a channel by allocating:
options: Different frequency bands to different signals
questions.solution: FDM places each signal in its own frequency band on the shared medium.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Instrumentation amplifier CMRR depends on:
questions.solution: CMRR = A_d/A_cm. Depends on R matching. 3-op-amp topology: high CMRR (100+ dB).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Channel — path loss:
options: Free space: L = (4πd/λ)². Log-distance: PL = PL₀ + 10n·log(d). n = path loss exponent
questions.solution: Path loss exponent: free space 2, urban 3-4, indoor 4-5, shadowed 5-6.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The Barkhausen criterion for sustained oscillation requires the loop gain to be:
options: Unity with zero net phase shift
questions.solution: Oscillation is sustained when |loop gain| = 1 and total phase around the loop = 0°/360°.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Op-amp — PSRR:
options: Power Supply Rejection Ratio. ΔV_os/ΔV_s. Rejects supply noise
questions.solution: PSRR: lower at high frequency. Decoupling caps (0.1μF) near each supply pin.

questions.subject: Electronics and Telecommunication Engineering
questions.stem: Switched-capacitor integrator: clock feedthrough due to:
questions.solution: When switch turns off, charge injects into integrating capacitor. Bottom-plate sampling or complementary switches reduce this.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A PID controller consists of:
questions.solution: PID: K_p + K_i/s + K_d·s. P reduces rise time, I eliminates SS error, D reduces overshoot.

questions.subject: Electronics and Telecommunication Engineering
questions.stem: Internal model principle: for zero steady-state error, controller must contain:
questions.solution: IMP: plant + controller must include dynamics of reference/disturbance. Type-1 for step, Type-2 for ramp, etc.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Companding in PCM:
options: Compresses dynamic range before quantizing (μ-law, A-law)
questions.solution: Companding: more quantization levels for small signals (higher SNR for low amplitudes).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Voltage follower (buffer) has gain of:
questions.solution: Buffer: V_out = V_in. Unity gain. Infinite input impedance, zero output impedance.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: OFDM:
options: Orthogonal frequency division multiplexing. Multiple subcarriers, cyclic prefix. Used in 4G/5G, WiFi
questions.solution: OFDM: FFT-based. Subcarrier orthogonality. CP eliminates ISI. PAPR issue. LTE: SC-FDMA uplink.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A MOSFET is preferred in digital ICs largely because of its:
options: Very high input impedance and low power
questions.solution: The insulated gate gives near-infinite input impedance and low static power.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Memory types — RAM:
options: SRAM (static, flip-flop, fast, low density). DRAM (capacitor, slow, high density, refresh needed)
questions.solution: SRAM: 6T cell. DRAM: 1T+1C. DRAM requires refresh (~64ms). SDRAM: synchronous.
